mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns exceed 55% during rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that drive negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances or signal fails to predict direction during historical unemployment spikes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in greater than 50% of cases or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates resilient supply-side capacity that outpaces demand destruction, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during tightening periods or Rank IC falls below 0.01 in regime-filtered walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index levels reflect persistent inflationary pressure that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns exceed 55% when CPIAUCSL is above its 12-month moving average or Rank IC remains negative across all test windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 200 thousand confirm robust economic momentum, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 200 thousand month-over-month
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative returns in greater than 60% of cases or signal loses predictive power when payroll growth exceeds 300 thousand.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth above 2.5 percent and CPIAUCSL above 3.0 percent signals an overheating economy that triggers aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint high-growth high-inflation periods yield positive returns in greater than 50% of cases or interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent indicates a sudden supply-side contraction that disrupts corporate output and triggers negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 0.5 percent month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of triggered instances or signal fails to predict downturns during historical supply shocks.
